cards.name: Block Walk
cards.description: Boots and a clipboard. The one play that never turns on you. When in doubt. In the general, doors become turnout.

cards.name: Phone Bank
cards.description: Half the yield, none of the weather. Grandma's kitchen table is HQ. In the general, the phone is a GOTV tool.

cards.name: Yard Sign Blitz
cards.description: A district that sees your name starts believing it belongs there.

cards.name: Petition Drive
cards.description: Signatures instead of a fee. Labor is the currency you were born holding.

cards.name: Pay the Filing Fee
cards.description: $1,250 and it's done. Shame-free, story-free.

cards.name: Town Hall
cards.description: Folding chairs, burnt coffee, real questions. The kids notice if you skip these.

cards.name: Candidate Forum
cards.description: Sixty seconds and every rival watching for the stumble.

cards.name: Kitchen-Table Meeting
cards.description: A chair's kitchen, her rules. Bring pie; leave with a precinct or nothing. (Primary circuit — out of the general.)

cards.name: Earned Media Pitch
cards.description: A county weekly, a drive-time host, a stringer if you're lucky.

cards.name: Press Release
cards.description: Nobody prints it. Everybody files it. The reporter learns your name spelling.

cards.name: Fish Fry
cards.description: Five-dollar plates, donation jar, casseroles. Net positive, always.

cards.name: Court the Chairs (Pie Circuit)
cards.description: The kitchen-table circuit at scale. Phase III chairs are already spoken for.

cards.name: Straw Poll Push
cards.description: Pack the room, count the hands. Clubs remember who wins their straw.

cards.name: Club Speech
cards.description: Rubber chicken, real gatekeepers. Read the room or the room reads you.

cards.name: Oppo Research
cards.description: A quiet man with a courthouse habit. What he finds becomes yours to spend — or to hold.

cards.name: Debate Prep
cards.description: The Kitchen Cabinet plays your rival better than your rival does.

cards.name: GOTV Weekend
cards.description: Rapport is a promise. Turnout is the promise kept. One volunteer and a weekend. The general spine.

cards.name: Rides to the Polls
cards.description: The truck runs routes. Steepest conversion where turnout is lowest. Needs the Flatbed (shop A06).

cards.name: Recruit Volunteers
cards.description: An army marches on casseroles. Every volunteer makes every other card better.

cards.name: Sharpen the Message
cards.description: You are not running on nine things. You are running on one thing, said so it stays said.

cards.name: Take the PAC Check
cards.description: A man in a good suit admires your race. The check is real. So is the string tied to it. Once. Session will collect.

cards.name: Self-Fund on Credit
cards.description: The bank will lend against the homestead. Campaigns have eaten better men's farms. Spend it now — the bill lands on the win/loss branch, not your odds.

cards.name: Contrast Mail
cards.description: What the quiet man found, printed on cheap stock and mailed to every primary voter who votes.

cards.name: Promote a Canvass Captain
cards.description: One volunteer stops showing up as a volunteer and starts showing up as staff.

cards.name: Hire a Field Director
cards.description: A professional who has run four of these before. Money buys what volunteers can't always deliver on schedule.

cards.name: See the Slate-Maker
cards.description: One man prints the card half the primary votes from. His price is never only money.

cards.name: Prayer Breakfast
cards.description: Biscuits at six-thirty. The Corridor watches who shows before sunrise.

cards.name: Coffee with the Editor
cards.description: Not for an endorsement — for a fair shake. The weekly decides who's "serious" long before the voters do.

cards.name: Court the County Judge
cards.description: The one endorsement that moves a whole county. He gives it to winners, so look like one.

cards.name: Attend the Funeral
cards.description: A beloved judge has died. You can be present, or you can be seen. Presence earns the living.

cards.name: Call in the Precinct Chair network
cards.description: The chairs you banked open a door: lists, volunteers, a quiet county nod. Special kit — Precinct Chair orbit.

cards.name: Execute the field plan
cards.description: The captain's route book becomes the week's law. Special kit — Canvass Captain orbit. Field AP and turf GOTV.

cards.name: Spend the courthouse nod
cards.description: The heaviest local name spends itself once. Special kit — County Judge orbit. Endorsement gravity and name heat.

cards.name: Activate the party apparatus
cards.description: The Chairwoman opens the file and the volunteer list. Special kit — County Party orbit.

cards.name: Pull the club roster
cards.description: Every name that votes straw. Special kit — Club Leader orbit.

cards.name: Call in the fair shake
cards.description: Not an endorsement — the benefit of the doubt in print. Special kit — Local Editor orbit.

cards.name: Open the corridor
cards.description: The Pastor's hand on both of yours. Directory and volunteers. Special kit — Faith Leader orbit.

cards.name: Run the slate hard
cards.description: Half the primary votes from a printed card. Special kit — Slate-Maker orbit. His marker was already on it.

cards.name: Call the finance book
cards.description: The Finance Chair opens the call sheet. Special kit — money spine, not a free weekly drip forever.

cards.name: Take the drive-time slot
cards.description: Open mic between farm reports and the noon news. Special kit — Radio Host orbit. Name heat, not an endorsement.

cards.name: Spend the access map
cards.description: A junior lobbyist with a conscience walks you the side door. Special kit — access, not a vote.

cards.name: Spend the plant-gate nod
cards.description: The local president puts a hand on your shoulder at shift change. Special kit — Union orbit. Volunteers and doors.

cards.name: Work the chicken circuit
cards.description: Rubber chicken, name tags, reliable voters. Special kit — Chamber orbit. Money and polite weight.

cards.name: Sit the feed-store bench
cards.description: Unofficial senate on the bench out front. Special kit — Feed-Store orbit. Rumor is infrastructure.

cards.name: Grocery-Store Handshakes
cards.description: An hour at the exit doors. Cheap, endless, and it actually works.

cards.name: Church Bulletin Ad
cards.description: A quarter page next to the potluck schedule. The congregation notices who shows up in print.

cards.name: Little-League Sponsorship
cards.description: Your name on forty small backs, every Saturday, all season. Turnout starts young — through the parents.

cards.name: Letter to the Editor
cards.description: Three hundred words, tightly argued. The people who vote in primaries read the paper.

cards.name: Coffee-Shop Sit-Down
cards.description: One table, four regulars, refills on the house. Retail politics at its most literal.

cards.name: Union Hall Endorsement
cards.description: The business agent likes you enough to put it in writing. Stewards walk turf you never could.

cards.name: Targeted Digital Buy
cards.description: Lookalike audiences, dayparted, A/B tested. The consultant swears by the dashboard.

cards.name: Charter the Senior Vans
cards.description: The seniors vote at ninety percent and love a ride. Turnout you can schedule.

cards.name: Opposition Tracker
cards.description: A kid with a camcorder at every rival event, waiting for the gaffe. Sometimes it comes.

cards.name: Megachurch Sunday
cards.description: Three thousand seats and a pastor who might, or might not, wave you up front.

cards.name: Statewide Figure Endorses
cards.description: A name people know from television deigns to say yours. The bump is real; so is the debt.

cards.name: The Viral Moment
cards.description: You say the exact right thing at the exact right second and the internet decides to care.

cards.name: Machine Turnout Blitz
cards.description: Every captain, every van, every list, one weekend. When the machine moves as one, the count moves with it.

cards.name: Ribbon-Cutting Circuit
cards.description: A new bridge, a clinic wing, a fire truck. You hold the giant scissors and the district sees you deliver.

cards.name: Interim Charity Gala
cards.description: A good cause, a ballroom, and every lobbyist in town buying a table. Goodwill and debts, exchanged over rubber chicken.

cards.name: Face Down the Primary Threat
cards.description: A challenger is testing the waters back home. You can ignore it — or plant your flag and stare them down.

cards.name: File the Bill
cards.description: Your name, your issue, a number. It exists now, which is more than most ideas get.

cards.name: Seek Referral
cards.description: Bills go where the Speaker sends them. (Opens wk 2.) If the PAC holds a claim, they collect before the desk moves.

cards.name: Refuse the PAC Call
cards.description: They want an aye on a quiet association bill. Refuse and keep your district — pay in heat and ink.

cards.name: Court the Chair
cards.description: The chair decides what gets heard. (Hearings open wk 4.) Kitchen-table rules, marble floors.

cards.name: Committee Testimony
cards.description: Witnesses, a timer, members reading their phones. (Votes-out open wk 6.)

cards.name: Beg a Calendar Slot
cards.description: Calendars decides what the House even sees. (Opens wk 9.) Favor is the only currency here.

cards.name: Floor Fight
cards.description: Amendments fly, points of order lurk, the back mic is loaded. (Floor opens wk 11.)

cards.name: Work the Senate
cards.description: Thirty-one senators, the Lt. Governor, and the Tag in wait. (Opens wk 13.)

cards.name: District Casework
cards.description: A veteran's benefits, a stop-sign petition, a widow's property line. The seat is kept here, not in Austin.

cards.name: The Speaker's Errand
cards.description: Carry a small unpleasant thing for leadership. It costs your name a little; it buys your bill a lot.

cards.name: Whip a Vote Trade
cards.description: Your aye for his, payable when called. The whole building runs on this ledger.

cards.name: Study the Rules
cards.description: Most members never read them. The ones who do own the ones who don't.

cards.name: Play the Writ
cards.description: One procedural miracle, pre-paid. Spend it where the session bends.

cards.name: Teach a Night Class
cards.description: Civics at the community college, two evenings a week. Thirty adults who now know your name and your handshake.

cards.name: Write the Weekly Column
cards.description: A standing byline in the county paper. Slow, unglamorous, and it keeps your name in print between the elections.

cards.name: Run the Charity 5K
cards.description: A bib, a starting pistol, and a banner with your name over the finish line. Good works, well photographed.

cards.name: Work the List
cards.description: Call the names that still pick up. The county forgets slower when you dial.

cards.name: Host an Issue Forum
cards.description: The candidate lost; the cause did not. Folding chairs and a sharp message.

cards.name: Carry the Bag
cards.description: Two years inside. Briefs, desks, and where the levers hide.

cards.name: Mend the Fence
cards.description: Fix what broke. Coach the team. Let the mailers fade a little.

cards.name: Lunch the Lobby
cards.description: Title still warm. Doors still open. No vote — only lunch.

cards.name: Keep the Rolodex Warm
cards.description: Birthdays, funerals, fish fries you are not running. Yet.

cards.name: Quiet Money
cards.description: A retainer, a speech fee, a board. Not a campaign — not yet.

cards.name: Draft the Brief
cards.description: White paper, memo, bill draft that waits for a sponsor.

cards.name: Test the Waters
cards.description: Quiet calls about a larger map. Senate row or the statewide ballot.

cards.name: Fill the Square
cards.description: You do not ask for the room. You fill it, and the turnout operation fills the rest.

cards.name: The Unbending Line
cards.description: The message arrives pre-sharpened and never wavers. You say the same true thing until it is the only thing.

cards.name: Call In a Marker
cards.description: Someone owes you, and today is the day. The angle was always there.

cards.name: The Airtight Filing
cards.description: Every box checked, every deadline beaten. The clerk finds nothing because there is nothing to find.

cards.name: Broker the Grand Bargain
cards.description: You seat the rancher next to the union man and both leave thinking they won.

cards.name: Remember Every Name
cards.description: The kid, the dog, the surgery last spring. You remember, and they never forget that you did.

cards.name: March on the Capitol Steps
cards.description: Conviction with muscle behind it — a crowd that showed up angry and organized.

cards.name: The Elbow
cards.description: Loud out front, and an elbow the establishment never sees coming.

cards.name: Outwork the Field
cards.description: Grind plus the rulebook. You are still knocking when the others go home.

cards.name: The Family Name Opens the Gate
cards.description: Your name means something here. The chairs already wave you through.

cards.name: The Homecoming
cards.description: Star quarterback, then feed-store owner, now this. The county has rooted for you for decades.

cards.name: Burn It Down to Build It Up
cards.description: A disciplined message and a knife for the primary. You are exactly as angry as you choose to be.

cards.name: File the Whistleblower Complaint
cards.description: A cause and the rulebook to advance it. You file, and the file has teeth.

cards.name: Cross the Aisle
cards.description: Steady, principled, trusted across the aisle — the kind they call "serious."

cards.name: Revival Weekend
cards.description: A pulpit is a precinct and Sundays are turnout. You move people, and you mean it.

cards.name: Work the System
cards.description: You know the rules AND how to bend them. Dangerous in a committee, deadly near a deadline.

cards.name: The Grand Trade
cards.description: Two of everything and a price on each. You can trade your way out of almost anything.

cards.name: Steal the Cycle
cards.description: Timing and charm: you know the line AND the moment to land it. Made for the cameras.

cards.name: The Speaker's Ear
cards.description: Process mastery and the relationships to use it. Leadership is watching this profile.

cards.name: Explain It Like It's Simple
cards.description: You explain the water-district budget so plainly people thank you for it.

cards.name: Call In the Family Rolodex
cards.description: A known name and a gift for people. Doors open on the family reputation; you keep them open on your own.

cards.name: Parent-Teacher Circuit
cards.description: Twenty years of cafeteria nights. You work the rooms that already know your name.

cards.name: Halls of Honor
cards.description: The VFW and Legion still stand when the cameras leave. Bio is armor; the halls are turnout.

cards.name: Call In the Store Credit
cards.description: Everyone still owes you a favor or a bag of feed. You cash favors before cash.

cards.name: Outsource Petition Drive to University Interns
cards.description: A hundred students, one afternoon, clipboards everywhere. Signatures at scale — the labor door, staffed by the young.

cards.name: Work the Bundler's List
cards.description: Someone else's rolodex, dialing for you. The money arrives bundled, with strings you can mostly ignore.

cards.name: Turn Out the Precinct Captains
cards.description: Every captain a small turnout operation. When they move together, the count moves with them.

cards.name: The Anchor Takes Your Call
cards.description: A friendly anchor and a standing invitation. When you have something to say, the county hears it that night.

cards.name: Stand Up a Turf Operation
cards.description: Captains, turfs, cut lists. The volunteers you recruited become a machine that runs without you.

cards.name: Become a Fixture
cards.description: You are not campaigning anymore; you are just around, the way the weather is around. People vote for the weather they know.

cards.name: Cash the Kingmaker's Chit
cards.description: The people who decide have decided it is your turn. One call, and the slate rearranges itself around you.

personas.name: The Veteran
personas.description: Two tours and a flag on the porch. Bio is armor.

personas.name: The Teacher
personas.description: Twenty years of parent-teacher nights. You know the rooms.

personas.name: The Preacher
personas.description: A pulpit is a precinct. Sundays are turnout.

personas.name: The Feed-Store Owner
personas.description: Everyone owes you credit or a favor.

personas.name: The Powerhouse
personas.description: A presence that fills a room and a turnout operation to match. You win by showing up bigger than anyone.

personas.name: The True Believer
personas.description: The message arrives pre-sharpened and never wavers. Discipline is your whole discipline.

personas.name: The Operator
personas.description: You know whose cousin owes whom, and when to move. The angle is always there if you look.

personas.name: The Parliamentarian
personas.description: You read the rules twice. Process is a weapon that never jams.

personas.name: The Coalition-Builder
personas.description: You can seat the rancher next to the union man and make them both feel heard. Rooms open for you.

personas.name: The Natural
personas.description: Every door opens a little wider. People just take to you, and you know exactly what to do with that.

personas.name: The Movement Champion
personas.description: Conviction with muscle behind it. You bring the crowd and you keep them in line.

personas.name: The Bare-Knuckle Populist
personas.description: You go out loud and you fight dirty when you must. The establishment never sees the elbow coming.

personas.name: The Workhorse
personas.description: You outwork everyone and you know the process cold. Grind plus rules is a hard thing to beat.

personas.name: The Rural Patriarch
personas.description: Your name means something here, and the chairs already wave. Two grounds start warm.

personas.name: The Local Legend
personas.tagline: the county's been rooting for you
personas.description: Star quarterback, then feed-store owner, now this. The county has been rooting for you for decades.

personas.name: The Insurgent
personas.description: A disciplined message and a knife for the primary. You are exactly as angry as you choose to be.

personas.name: The Reform Crusader
personas.description: A cause and the rulebook to advance it. Straight Shooter before week one.

personas.name: The Statesman
personas.description: Steady, principled, trusted across the aisle. The kind they call "serious."

personas.name: The Fixer
personas.description: You know the rules AND how to bend them. Dangerous in a committee, deadly near a deadline.

personas.name: The Wheeler-Dealer
personas.description: Two of everything and a price on each. You can trade your way out of almost anything.

personas.name: The Showman
personas.description: Timing and charm: you know the line AND the moment to land it. Made for the cameras.

personas.name: The Committee Chair-in-Waiting
personas.description: Process mastery and the relationships to use it. Leadership is watching this profile.

personas.name: The Homegrown Wonk
personas.description: You explain the water district budget so plainly people thank you for it. Smart and likeable is rare.

personas.name: The Dealmaker's Heir
personas.description: A known name and a gift for people. Doors open on the family reputation; you keep them open on your own.

issues.name: Property taxes
issues.description: Appraisal districts, school M&O, the levy that never sleeps.

issues.name: Water rights
issues.description: Groundwater districts, river authorities, and drought maps.

issues.name: The border
issues.description: Federal failure, local consequence. Easy to shout; hard to govern.

issues.name: Rural hospitals
issues.description: OB deserts, ambulance miles, and the last ER light.

issues.name: Highway tolls
issues.description: They promised the tolls would come off when the road was paid. The road is paid.

issues.name: Teacher pay
issues.description: Twenty years in a classroom and a second job at the feed store. The room already agrees; make it vote.

issues.name: Ag subsidies & crop insurance
issues.description: One hailstorm from foreclosure, every single year. The FM roads know the arithmetic.

issues.name: Courthouse corruption
issues.description: The commissioners' court has been a family business for forty years. Naming it takes nerve.

issues.name: Rural broadband
issues.description: Kids do homework in the church parking lot for the wifi. The future has a dead zone.

issues.name: Prison & bail reform
issues.description: The unit is the county's biggest employer and its heaviest silence. Careful, serious ground.

issues.name: Mental health funding
issues.description: The sheriff runs the largest psychiatric facility in three counties: his jail. Even he says so.

issues.name: Veterans' services
issues.description: The Legion hall knows every name on the waiting list. Show up and listen first.

issues.name: Rural grid reliability
issues.description: Everyone remembers the freeze. Every generator in every barn is a campaign memorial.

issues.name: Payday lending
issues.description: Four storefronts by the plant gate, 400% APR. The math preys on shift workers by design.

issues.name: Public school vouchers
issues.description: The church wants them; the small towns fear them — the district IS the school. A knife-edge issue.

issues.name: Election integrity
issues.description: The county clerk is tired, honest, and yelled at from both directions. Order-flavored, radioactive, real.

districts.name: Safe district, entrenched incumbent
districts.description: Twelve years and a war chest.
